partial_issues: The claim states 'ob_checkpoint_seq[m] <= recovered = ob_seq[m]-1' as a post-condition invariant. This holds because MinOfTwo(@, recovered) produces min(old_checkpoint, recovered), and recovered = ob_seq'-1 by construction. Verified through double-crash, triple-crash, and fresh-start scenarios.

concerns: P1 (TypeInvariant): ob_checkpoint_seq[m] \in 0..MaxSeq added to TypeInvariant. MinOfTwo of two values in 0..MaxSeq stays in 0..MaxSeq. Init sets 0. OBCheckpoint sets ob_seq[m]-1 which is in 0..MaxSeq since ob_seq[m] > 1 (guard) and ob_seq[m] <= MaxSeq+1 (TypeInvariant). Safe.

P3 (OBSequenceMonotonic): No seq assignment changed for production actions. OBViewChange sets ob_seq = recovered+1, same structure as before. The anchor change only affects the recovered value, which is still computed via MaxContiguousFrom on contiguous archiver data. No two effects in the same session can share a seq. Safe.
P10 (OBLogBounded): Log pruned to {e: e.seq <= recovered}, and ob_seq = recovered+1. All retained entries have seq <= recovered < recovered+1 = ob_seq. Safe.
P5 (OBNamespaceSeparation): OBCheckpoint touches only ob_checkpoint_seq. No type mixing introduced. Safe.
P13 (OBBackupAppliedValid): backup_applied cleared on view change, OBCheckpoint doesn't touch it. Safe.
P14 (OBIndependenceFromCE): ob_checkpoint_seq is purely OB-local, no CE dependency. Safe.
P15 (CEPendingRecoveryBounded): ob_pending_seq reset to 1 on view change, unaffected. Safe.

hidden_regressions: MINOR (design-level, not spec-correctness): OBCheckpoint can set ob_checkpoint_seq to ob_seq[m]-1 before archivers have ingested that seq. However, this is not a spec bug because: (a) the local ob_log persists across OBCrash (UNCHANGED ob_log), so entries below checkpoint survive in the log even without archiver backup, and (b) the MinOfTwo cap in OBViewChange ensures checkpoint never exceeds recovered, which is derived from actual archiver availability. The checkpoint is therefore a safe local hint, not an archiver-side contract.

MINOR: OBCheckpoint can fire multiple times per session, each time overwriting the checkpoint to ob_seq[m]-1. This is monotonically non-decreasing within a session (since ob_seq only increases), so repeated checkpointing is safe. No idempotency issue.

issues: Verified: ob_checkpoint_seq added to obVars tuple. All actions using UNCHANGED <<...obVars...>> automatically cover it (OBBackupApply, all non-OB actions). Actions that enumerate individual ob_ variables (OBProduceEffect, OBProducePendingAction, OBReceiveMFR) have ob_checkpoint_seq added to their UNCHANGED. OBCrash adds ob_checkpoint_seq to UNCHANGED (checkpoint persists across crash, which is correct since the cap happens in OBViewChange). OBCheckpoint modifies it. OBViewChange caps it via MinOfTwo. All accounted for.
Verified: OBCheckpoint appears in Next under the \E m \in Markets quantifier.
Verified: The @ reference in [ob_checkpoint_seq EXCEPT ![m] = MinOfTwo(@, recovered)] correctly refers to ob_checkpoint_seq[m] in TLA+ EXCEPT syntax.

summary: The revision correctly fixes the cross-session staleness bug identified in the original review. The MinOfTwo(ob_checkpoint_seq[m], recovered) cap in OBViewChange ensures the checkpoint never exceeds the recovered watermark, preventing the sequence gap that would strand downstream OMS consumers. Traced through double-crash, triple-crash, fresh-start, and archiver-lag scenarios; all produce stable, gap-free recovery. The local ob_log persistence across OBCrash (UNCHANGED ob_log) is the key enabler: even when archivers lag behind the checkpoint, the log retains the entries locally, and MaxContiguousFrom correctly computes recovery from actual archiver availability. All UNCHANGED clauses are correct, OBCheckpoint is properly guarded and appears in Next, and all seven local/spanning properties are preserved.
